title: IS NULL predicate not correctly processed with DeltaLake table
description:
### Describe the bug
Easy to reproduce.
### How to reproduce
Create the table with [spark.py](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22464030/spark.py). Then in ClickHouse run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Dynamic) ENGINE = DeltaLakeLocal(local, format = 'Parquet', path = '/var/lib/clickhouse/user_files/lakehouses/spark_catalog/test/t0');
SELECT count() FROM numbers(1) x RIGHT JOIN t0 ON number = c0 WHERE NOT c0 IS NULL;
-- 0 wrong, it should match the number of rows in t0
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
poc: CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Dynamic) ENGINE = DeltaLakeLocal(local, format = 'Parquet', path = '/var/lib/clickhouse/user_files/lakehouses/spark_catalog/test/t0');
key_bug_features: IS NULL predicate processing with DeltaLake table
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id UInt32, value Nullable(String)) ENGINE = DeltaLakeLocal(local, format = 'Parquet', path = '/var/lib/clickhouse/user_files/lakehouses/spark_catalog/test/t1');
CREATE TABLE t2 (num UInt32, description Nullable(String)) ENGINE = DeltaLakeLocal(local, format = 'Parquet', path = '/var/lib/clickhouse/user_files/lakehouses/spark_catalog/test/t2');
SELECT COUNT() FROM t1 RIGHT JOIN t2 ON t1.id = t2.num WHERE NOT t1.value IS NULL;
